event: The Meeting of Anne and Joachim at the Golden Gate
symbols: embrace of the elderly couple — the miraculous reunion after divine intervention
clasped hands — covenant faithfulness and marital love restored by God's promise
Joachim's aged face — years of barrenness and waiting now rewarded
Anne's white head covering — purity and faithful endurance
description: A close-up fresco detail showing two elderly figures in an intimate embrace, clasping each other's hands and leaning their faces close together. On the left, a bearded old man with white hair wears a golden-yellow cloak over blue garments. On the right, a woman with a white head covering gazes intently at the man. The background shows a distant landscape with hills and figures. The emotional intensity of the meeting is conveyed through the tender physical closeness and the earnest expressions of both figures.
theological_description: This scene depicts the legendary meeting of Joachim and Anne at the Golden Gate of Jerusalem, a story from the Protoevangelium of James (an apocryphal text). According to tradition, Joachim and Anne were an elderly, childless couple — Joachim had been turned away from the Temple because his barrenness was seen as a sign of divine disfavor. After both received angelic announcements that Anne would conceive, they met joyfully at the Golden Gate. While apocryphal, this narrative echoes authentic biblical patterns of divine intervention in barrenness: Sarah and Abraham (Genesis 18:10-14), Hannah and Elkanah (1 Samuel 1:19-20), and Elizabeth and Zechariah (Luke 1:13). The embrace of Joachim and Anne prefigures the Visitation of Mary and Elizabeth (Luke 1:39-45) — elderly parents receiving impossible children by divine grace. The tradition holds that Anne conceived Mary, the future Mother of God, making this meeting a prelude to the entire drama of salvation.

scripture_refs.reason: The angel's announcement to elderly Zechariah that Elizabeth will bear a son — the closest biblical parallel to Joachim and Anne's story
The promise to aged Abraham and Sarah — the biblical archetype of miraculous conception in old age
Hannah's barrenness ended by divine grace — another biblical parallel to Joachim and Anne

_meta.title: The Meeting of Anne and Joachim at the Golden Gate (detail)
